title: Fast embedding generation with parallel workers
story: As a framework developer, I want embedding generation to use multiple CPU cores so that regeneration completes in under 10 minutes instead of 55.
acceptance_criteria: Worker pool distributes chunks across N threads
Crashed workers are respawned and failed batches retried

title: Hardware-accelerated embeddings
story: As a framework user on Apple Silicon, I want the embedding pipeline to use my Neural Engine/GPU automatically so that I get maximum performance without manual configuration.
acceptance_criteria: Auto-detects Apple Silicon and uses CoreML
Auto-detects NVIDIA GPU and uses CUDA
Falls back to CPU gracefully on any platform

title: Configurable performance knobs
story: As a power user, I want to tune parallelism, device, batch size, and model precision through config.json or CLI flags so that I can optimize for my specific hardware.
acceptance_criteria: CLI flags override config for one-off runs
Sensible defaults when nothing is configured

title: Progress visibility during generation
story: As a developer waiting for embeddings, I want to see chunks/sec, ETA, and worker utilization so that I know how long the generation will take.
